title: Severe Hypertension - Hypertensive Emergency
chief_complaint: Severe headache, blurred vision, and confusion for 6 hours. Known hypertension, stopped medications 2 weeks ago.

physical_exam.neuro: Alert but confused, fundoscopy shows papilledema and retinal hemorrhages

correct_diagnosis: Hypertensive Emergency with Acute Kidney Injury and Hypertensive Retinopathy
key_learning_points: Lower BP by 25% in first hour, not to normal
Avoid rapid BP lowering (risk of cerebral hypoperfusion)

patient_responses.medications: I ran out of my blood pressure pills 2 weeks ago
patient_responses.headache: It's the worst headache of my life
patient_responses.vision: My vision is blurry, like looking through fog

hints.management: IV antihypertensive, lower by 25% in first hour

feedback.incorrect_message: This is hypertensive emergency. The BP + end-organ damage (AKI, retinopathy) confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Hypertensive emergency: IV labetalol 20mg bolus then 1-2mg/min, or nicardipine 5mg/hr. Lower MAP by 25% in first hour. Target 160/100 in 24h. Do NOT lower to normal rapidly. Admit to ICU.
